title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Wano Citizens, Unknown person #8 as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Get a real job, Kin'Emon!! If you stay in the Yakuza...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: You will probably end up dead!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Fine by me!! Nobody would care if I died, anyway!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ouch!! Why did you throw a rock at me?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Stop it, boy!!! If you hurt the mountain god...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I WILL SLICE OPEN YOUR STOMACH, YOU BEAST!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WHAT KIND OF GOD are you!!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Then I have no right to continue living!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey, Boar!! Is this what you have been looking for!?

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 30 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
